name: The Big Three — a reflection casting
description: A three-position casting that mirrors the tarot three-card spread so it works in the oracle from day one — but nothing here is drawn at random: every position resolves to an aspect of YOUR OWN chart (Sun, Moon, Ascendant). The chart is a mirror and a calendar, never a command: each position hands you a prompt about something already yours, and you author what it means. Positions are read against the flagship interpretation set (western-astrology-canonical) or any interpretation grammar the reader chooses (the historical voices work too — Ptolemy, Leo, the skeptics).

casting._note: NEVER random: each position derives from the querent's chart. Sun and Moon sign need only the birth date(time); the Ascendant needs time + place and a real chart calculation (ephemeris — see docs/RECURSIVE-ECO-INTEGRATION.md §ephemeris; the family's engine is the Skyfield backend noted in nara's assets inventory). Data-only fallback: the reader enters placements they already know. Resolution: each position binds to the matching item of the `from` grammar (planet item + the sign it occupies; ascendant → the rising sign + house-1).

sections.Position: The first mirror: the Sun — where your conscious life points. Resolved from your chart: the sign (and, with a full chart, the house) your Sun occupies.
sections.Prompt: Read the Sun item and your Sun-sign item side by side. What in this description is already visibly at work in your life — and what does it invite that you have been postponing? Hold it loosely: you decide what fits.

sections.Position: The second mirror: the Moon — the habits of feeling, what comforts and what unsettles. Resolved from your chart: the sign your Moon occupies.
sections.Prompt: Where the Sun position asked what you are building, this one asks what you are protecting. Read your Moon placement as a description of your default weather — then ask: is this weather, or is it a wall? The answer is yours, not the chart's.

sections.Position: The third mirror: the Ascendant — the sign rising on the eastern horizon at your birth; the threshold between you and the room. Needs birth time + place (a real chart calculation), or enter it if you already know it.
sections.Prompt: The rising sign describes an entrance, not an essence. Read it and ask: what do people meet first in me — and is that a door I open on purpose, or one that swings on its own? Neither answer is wrong; noticing is the reading.
